title: PwD / Senior Citizen Guide
description: Information on Home Voting, Saksham app, and special polling station facilities.

start.content: Are you checking for yourself or someone else who is an 85+ senior citizen or a Person with Disability (PwD)?

options.label: I just want to know about booth facilities

home_voting.content: Senior citizens above 85 years of age are eligible for the Home Voting facility. You need to fill out **Form 12D** within 5 days of the election notification.

pwd_options.content: Are you marked as a PwD voter in the electoral roll?

pwd_unmarked.content: You can register yourself as a PwD voter using the Saksham ECI App or the Voter Services Portal. This ensures special facilities are arranged for you.

pwd_marked.content: As a registered PwD voter with a 40% benchmark disability, you are eligible for the Home Voting facility. Submit **Form 12D** within 5 days of notification. Alternatively, you can use special facilities at the polling booth.

booth_facilities.content:
If visiting the polling station, these facilities are available:

- Assured Minimum Facilities (AMF) including ramps and drinking water.
- Wheelchairs on request.
- Priority voting (no need to stand in line).
- Free transport facility (can be booked via Saksham App).
- Braille on EVMs and Braille voter slips for visually impaired voters.
